Description: Stabilize live classroom core architecture by introducing RTC adapter boundary, orchestrator state machine, and participant registry for multi-user sessions.

Summary: Network Resilience and Adaptive Media Quality
Description: Implement policy-driven quality adaptation, dual-stream prioritization, and reconnect grace behavior for weak/unstable networks.
Deliver tutor-first classroom UX with stable state messaging, layout manager, and reliable screenshare behavior.
Enable classroom participant authorization and enrollment pipeline using Supabase and Next.js token APIs.
Add telemetry, QA reliability suite, and release quality gates for classroom production readiness.
Create Agora adapter layer to isolate SDK operations and emit normalized RTC events for orchestrator consumption.
All media operations used by session flow must be called via adapter API, not directly from screens/widgets.

Summary: Normalize Agora callbacks into typed RtcEvent stream
Description: Join/leave/audio/video/speaking callbacks are emitted as typed events and covered by unit tests.
Implement deterministic lifecycle transitions and route RTC events through reducer logic.
Support joining, connected, degraded, reconnecting, resumed, ended, failed with deterministic transitions.

Summary: Wire reducer from RtcEvent to ClassroomState
Description: UI no longer mutates session state directly from raw callbacks.
Replace single remote UID assumptions with participant map model.
No critical session rendering path depends on a single remote UID.

Summary: Add participant add/update/remove and grace tests
Description: Tests cover join, leave, reconnect grace, and state consistency edge cases.
Build quality tiers with hysteresis and metric-driven policy decisions.
Parent: Network Resilience and Adaptive Media Quality

Summary: Create quality_controller.dart with tiering and hysteresis
Description: Tier transitions require sustained evidence; no rapid oscillation under noisy metrics.
Controller receives periodic metrics and emits quality transition events.

Summary: Enable Dual Stream and Spotlight Switching
Description: Use high/low stream selection by participant priority to reduce stutter and bandwidth pressure.
Parent: Network Resilience and Adaptive Media Quality

Summary: Enable dual stream and tier-specific encoder profiles
Description: Dual stream mode verified in logs; profiles applied by quality tier.
Parent: Enable Dual Stream and Spotlight Switching

Summary: Implement spotlight policy for stream selection
Description: Tutor or spotlight participant uses high stream; non-focus participants use low stream.
Parent: Enable Dual Stream and Spotlight Switching

Summary: Add Reconnection Grace and UX Escalation
Description: Avoid abrupt tile removal and provide progressive reconnect UX messaging.
Parent: Network Resilience and Adaptive Media Quality

Description: Tiles remain with reconnecting overlay during grace window before definitive removal.
Parent: Add Reconnection Grace and UX Escalation

Summary: Implement reconnect message escalation by duration
Description: 0-3s silent retry, 3-10s reconnecting banner, >10s warning guidance.
Parent: Add Reconnection Grace and UX Escalation

Description: Expose clear joining/degraded/reconnecting/resumed/ended states without flicker.

Summary: Add state banners/chips with minimum display duration
Description: Users always see consistent session state and no visual thrashing under short events.

Summary: Verify status bar contrast on key onboarding/session screens
Description: Status bar icons and text remain visible across light and dark app bars.
Build spotlight + grid classroom layout with stable transitions.
Layout remains stable as participants join/leave and orientation changes.

Summary: Stabilize local preview and PiP during transitions
Description: Local preview and PiP do not disappear or jump on layout updates.
Ensure screenshare ownership, transitions, and fallback behavior are consistent.
Only one active share owner at a time; ownership transitions propagate to all clients.

Summary: Add degraded share fallback and recovery behavior
Description: When share stream degrades, UI communicates fallback and recovers to camera stage cleanly.
Support session_participants membership in token and role resolution endpoints.

Summary: Apply/verify session_participants migration in all envs
Description: Migration is deployed and verified in local/staging/prod with integrity checks.
Legacy participants and session_participants members can join; unauthorized users receive 403.

Summary: Build Enrollment Pipeline for Classroom Participants
Description: Auto-create participant rows for multi-learner sessions using user ids and roles.

Summary: Create enrollment writer and idempotency guard
Description: Multi-learner bookings reliably create session participant rows without duplicates.
Parent: Build Enrollment Pipeline for Classroom Participants

Summary: Add backfill script for existing eligible sessions
Description: Historical eligible sessions are populated safely with participant records.
Parent: Build Enrollment Pipeline for Classroom Participants

Description: Capture tier changes, reconnect outcomes, stream switches, and freeze events per session.
QoE schema is deployed with query-ready indexes and retention strategy.

Summary: Emit orchestrator QoE events to backend
Description: All core QoE events are persisted and traceable by session id.
Create reproducible scenario suite and release thresholds for classroom quality.

Summary: Build reliability matrix for 1:1, 1:3, 1:5 and degraded network
Description: Suite defines pass/fail outcomes and captures regression evidence.

Summary: Define release gate metrics and triage workflow
Description: Release blocked if reconnect/freeze thresholds fail; owners and response paths documented.
